 {"id":"bd-c9r","title":"Add tracing/logging infrastructure for debugging","description":"## Purpose
Add structured logging throughout sqlmodel-console using the tracing crate for debugging and observability. This enables detailed logging that can be enabled during testing and development.

## Background
Console operations can fail silently or behave unexpectedly. Structured logging:
- Helps diagnose issues in CI/CD
- Enables test output inspection
- Provides visibility into mode detection
- Tracks rendering performance

## Implementation Details

### File Location
crates/sqlmodel-console/src/logging.rs

### Dependencies to Add (Cargo.toml)
```toml
[dependencies]
tracing = "0.1"

[dev-dependencies]
tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter", "fmt"] }
tracing-test = "0.2"
```

### Core Implementation
```rust
//! Logging infrastructure for sqlmodel-console.
//!
//! All logging is done via tracing spans and events, allowing flexible
//! subscriber configuration for different environments.

use tracing::{debug, info, instrument, span, trace, warn, Level};

/// Initialize a test subscriber that captures logs.
#[cfg(test)]
pub fn init_test_logging() {
    use tracing_subscriber::{fmt, EnvFilter};
    let _ = fmt()
        .with_env_filter(EnvFilter::from_default_env()
            .add_directive("sqlmodel_console=trace".parse().unwrap()))
        .with_test_writer()
        .try_init();
}

/// Log level for console operations.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum LogLevel {
    /// Detailed trace information
    Trace,
    /// Debug information
    Debug,
    /// General information
    Info,
    /// Warnings
    Warn,
    /// Errors
    Error,
}

// Instrumented helper functions for key operations

/// Log mode detection with all relevant context.
#[instrument(level = "debug")]
pub fn log_mode_detection(
    env_vars: &[(& str, Option<String>)],
    is_tty: bool,
    detected_mode: &str,
) {
    debug!(
        env_vars = ?env_vars,
        is_tty = is_tty,
        detected_mode = detected_mode,
        "Output mode detection complete"
    );
}

/// Log renderable creation.
#[instrument(level = "trace", skip(renderable_type))]
pub fn log_renderable_created(renderable_type: &str, config: &str) {
    trace!(
        renderable = renderable_type,
        config = config,
        "Renderable created"
    );
}

/// Log render operation timing.
#[instrument(level = "debug", skip(output_len))]
pub fn log_render_complete(renderable_type: &str, width: usize, output_len: usize, duration_us: u64) {
    debug!(
        renderable = renderable_type,
        width = width,
        output_bytes = output_len,
        duration_us = duration_us,
        "Render complete"
    );
}

/// Log stream output.
#[instrument(level = "trace")]
pub fn log_stream_write(stream: &str, bytes: usize) {
    trace!(stream = stream, bytes = bytes, "Stream write");
}
```

### Usage in Mode Detection (mode.rs)
```rust
use crate::logging::log_mode_detection;

impl OutputMode {
    pub fn detect() -> Self {
        let env_vars = vec![
            ("SQLMODEL_PLAIN", std::env::var("SQLMODEL_PLAIN").ok()),
            ("CLAUDE_CODE", std::env::var("CLAUDE_CODE").ok()),
            // ... other vars
        ];
        let is_tty = std::io::stdout().is_terminal();
        
        let mode = // ... detection logic
        
        log_mode_detection(&env_vars, is_tty, &format!("{:?}", mode));
        mode
    }
}
```

### Usage in Console (console.rs)
```rust
use crate::logging::{log_stream_write, log_render_complete};
use std::time::Instant;

impl SqlModelConsole {
    pub fn print(&self, message: &str) {
        let start = Instant::now();
        // ... print logic
        log_stream_write("stdout", message.len());
    }
    
    pub fn print_renderable<R>(&self, renderable: &R) {
        let start = Instant::now();
        // ... render logic
        log_render_complete(
            std::any::type_name::<R>(),
            self.width(),
            output.len(),
            start.elapsed().as_micros() as u64,
        );
    }
}
```

## Test Integration
```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use tracing_test::traced_test;

    #[traced_test]
    #[test]
    fn test_mode_detection_logs() {
        let _ = OutputMode::detect();
        assert!(logs_contain("Output mode detection complete"));
    }

    #[traced_test]
    #[test]
    fn test_render_logs_timing() {
        let console = SqlModelConsole::with_mode(OutputMode::Plain);
        console.print("test");
        assert!(logs_contain("Stream write"));
    }
}
```

## Environment Variable for Log Level
RUST_LOG=sqlmodel_console=debug cargo test

## Verification
```bash
RUST_LOG=sqlmodel_console=trace cargo test -p sqlmodel-console -- --nocapture
cargo test -p sqlmodel-console logging::tests
```

## Why tracing Over log?
- Structured events with typed fields
- Span-based timing built in
- Better async support (future-proof)
- Test integration via tracing-test crate
- Industry standard in Rust ecosystem","acceptance_criteria":"tracing subscriber configured for console output
Log levels filter console verbosity appropriately
Structured fields captured for debugging
Log output respects OutputMode (colorized vs plain)
All unit tests verify logging behavior
Integration with existing sqlmodel logging works","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:24:08.191066251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:15:17.423123807Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T09:15:17.422997419Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-c9r","depends_on_id":"bd-1ob","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-c9r","depends_on_id":"bd-25i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-cc9","title":"Create Agent Compatibility Guide for AI coding tools","description":"## Purpose
Write detailed documentation for AI coding agent authors and users, explaining how sqlmodel-console maintains compatibility with automated tools.

## Background
AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex CLI, Cursor, Gemini CLI) are primary users of sqlmodel_rust. This guide ensures:
- Agent authors understand the output contract
- Users know how to configure for agents
- Developers know how to maintain compatibility

## Implementation Details

### File Location
docs/console/agent-compatibility.md

### Document Structure

#### 1. Why Agent Compatibility Matters
- AI agents parse stdout/stderr
- Rich formatting breaks parsing
- Agent detection prevents issues
- Stream separation preserves semantics

#### 2. How Auto-Detection Works
Environment variables checked (in order):
1. CLAUDE_CODE - Claude Code CLI
2. CODEX_CLI - OpenAI Codex CLI
3. CURSOR_SESSION - Cursor IDE
4. GEMINI_CLI - Google Gemini CLI
5. CODY_AGENT - Sourcegraph Cody
6. AIDER_MODE - Aider
7. CI - Generic CI environment

Detection logic:
- If any agent env var set: Plain mode
- If not a TTY (piped): Plain mode
- Otherwise: Rich mode

#### 3. Stream Separation Contract
stdout (semantic):
- Query results (rows, columns)
- Success/failure status codes
- Structured data (JSON if requested)

stderr (decorative):
- Progress indicators
- Connection status
- Timing information
- Formatted errors (visual version)

#### 4. Plain Text Output Format
Errors:
ERROR: Connection timeout (SQLMODEL-E001)
  Host: localhost:5432
  Timeout: 30s

Progress:
Inserting rows: 50% (500/1000) 123 rows/s

Results:
id | name  | email
1  | Alice | alice@example.com
2  | Bob   | bob@example.com
(2 rows)

#### 5. Environment Variables Reference
| Variable | Values | Effect |
|----------|--------|--------|
| CLAUDE_CODE | any | Force plain mode |
| SQLMODEL_PLAIN | 1 | Force plain mode |
| SQLMODEL_FORCE_RICH | 1 | Force rich mode |
| SQLMODEL_NO_COLOR | 1 | Disable colors |

#### 6. Testing Agent Compatibility
# Run in plain mode
SQLMODEL_PLAIN=1 cargo run --example query

# Simulate Claude Code
CLAUDE_CODE=1 cargo run --example query

# Verify no ANSI codes
SQLMODEL_PLAIN=1 cargo run --example query 2>&1 | cat -v

#### 7. For Agent Authors
Recommended detection:
- Set your unique env var
- Register it with us (open issue)
- Document for your users

Parsing recommendations:
- Read stdout for data
- Optionally display stderr to user
- Handle both rich and plain gracefully

#### 8. Maintaining Compatibility (for contributors)
Rules:
1. Never put semantic data in stderr
2. Plain mode must have zero ANSI codes
3. All new features need plain output
4. Test in CI (non-TTY environment)

## Verification Steps
1. All env vars documented
2. Stream contract clear
3. Plain format parseable
4. Agent detection accurate
5. Testing instructions work
6. Contributor guidelines clear

## Dependencies
- OutputMode detection finalized
- All agent env vars known
- Stream separation tested","acceptance_criteria":"Guide lists all supported AI coding agents
Guide explains detection mechanism
Guide shows how to force modes manually
Guide includes examples for each agent
Guide is linked from main README
Guide follows project documentation style","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:17:34.477706058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T07:02:11.972703789Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T07:02:11.972579898Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cc9","depends_on_id":"bd-2sh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-cc9","depends_on_id":"bd-amu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-cos","title":"Update main README with console feature documentation","description":"## Purpose
Update the main sqlmodel README.md to document the console feature, including setup instructions, screenshots, and links to detailed documentation.

## Background
The main README is often the first thing users see. It needs to:
- Mention the console feature exists
- Show what it looks like (screenshots)
- Provide quick setup instructions
- Link to detailed documentation

## Implementation Details

### File Modified
README.md (root of sqlmodel_rust)

### New Section: Console Output

Add after Features section:

## Console Output

SQLModel Rust includes optional rich console output for beautiful terminal feedback.

### Features
- Styled error messages with context
- Formatted query result tables
- Schema visualization trees
- Progress bars for bulk operations
- Agent-safe: auto-detects AI coding tools

### Quick Setup

Add the console feature:
cargo add sqlmodel --features console

Enable console on your session:
(code example)

### Screenshots

(Include 3-4 screenshots showing:)
- Error panel with styled output
- Query results table
- Schema tree visualization
- Progress bar during bulk insert

### Agent Compatibility

Console output is agent-safe by default. When running under Claude Code,
Codex CLI, or other AI coding tools, output automatically switches to
plain text that agents can parse.

Learn more: (link to agent guide)

### Disable Console

If you dont need console output:
cargo add sqlmodel  # without console feature

### Documentation
- Console User Guide
- Agent Compatibility Guide
- API Reference

### Screenshots Preparation
1. Run visual examples
2. Capture terminal screenshots
3. Both dark and light themes
4. Various terminal widths
5. Optimize images for web

### README Updates Checklist
- Add Console Output section
- Add screenshots
- Update Features list
- Add to Table of Contents
- Update installation section
- Add to Optional Features list

## Verification Steps
1. README renders correctly on GitHub
2. Screenshots display properly
3. Code examples compile
4. Links work
5. Information accurate
6. Consistent with other docs

## Dependencies
- Visual examples complete
- Screenshots captured
- API stable
- Feature fully working","acceptance_criteria":"README includes console feature in feature list
README shows basic console usage example
README links to detailed console documentation
README explains feature flags for console
README is consistent with other sections
Changes reviewed for accuracy","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:17:48.813117873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T07:03:06.263494187Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T07:03:06.263365557Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cos","depends_on_id":"bd-2sh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-cos","depends_on_id":"bd-cc9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-cos","depends_on_id":"bd-pqg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-cqi7","title":"Implement JSON/JSONB type support","description":"## Description

Full JSON type support with querying capabilities.

## Python Behavior

```python
from sqlalchemy import JSON
from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import JSONB

class Config(SQLModel, table=True):
    settings: dict = Field(sa_type=JSON)
    metadata: dict = Field(sa_type=JSONB)  # PostgreSQL
    
# Query into JSON
stmt = select(Config).where(Config.settings['key'] == 'value')
```

## Rust Implementation

```rust
use serde_json::Value as JsonValue;

#[derive(Model)]
struct Config {
    #[sqlmodel(sql_type = "JSON")]
    settings: JsonValue,
    
    #[sqlmodel(sql_type = "JSONB")]  // PostgreSQL
    metadata: JsonValue,
}

// JSON path queries
select!(Config)
    .filter(Config::settings.json_extract("key").eq("value"))
```

### Dialect Differences

- PostgreSQL: Native JSONB with operators
- MySQL: JSON with JSON_EXTRACT()
- SQLite: TEXT with json_extract()

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] JSON type mapping
- [ ] JSONB for PostgreSQL
- [ ] serde_json::Value serialization
- [ ] JSON path queries
- [ ] Dialect-specific SQL

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (crates/sqlmodel-core/src/types/json_type.rs)
- [ ] Test serde_json::Value to JSON/JSONB
- [ ] Test typed struct to JSONB
- [ ] Test JSON path extraction
- [ ] Test JSON containment operators
- [ ] Test invalid JSON error

### E2E Tests (tests/e2e/json_types.rs)
- [ ] Insert JSON object → PostgreSQL JSONB
- [ ] Query JSONB → serde_json::Value
- [ ] JSONB containment query (@>)
- [ ] JSONB path query (->>, #>>)
- [ ] Index on JSONB field (GIN)
- [ ] Null vs JSON null

### Logging
- [ ] DEBUG: JSON type detection
- [ ] TRACE: JSON serialization details

Full JSON type support with querying capabilities.

## Python Behavior

```python
from sqlalchemy import JSON
from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import JSONB

class Config(SQLModel, table=True):
    settings: dict = Field(sa_type=JSON)
    metadata: dict = Field(sa_type=JSONB)  # PostgreSQL
    
# Query into JSON
stmt = select(Config).where(Config.settings['key'] == 'value')
```

## Rust Implementation

```rust
use serde_json::Value as JsonValue;

#[derive(Model)]
struct Config {
    #[sqlmodel(sql_type = "JSON")]
    settings: JsonValue,
    
    #[sqlmodel(sql_type = "JSONB")]  // PostgreSQL
    metadata: JsonValue,
}

// JSON path queries
select!(Config)
    .filter(Config::settings.json_extract("key").eq("value"))
```

### Dialect Differences

- PostgreSQL: Native JSONB with operators
- MySQL: JSON with JSON_EXTRACT()
- SQLite: TEXT with json_extract()

 {"id":"bd-d7m","title":"Create ErrorPanel renderable in sqlmodel-console","description":"# Create ErrorPanel Renderable in sqlmodel-console

## Task Description

Create an ErrorPanel renderable specifically designed for displaying errors with
rich formatting in Rich mode and structured plain text in Plain mode.

## File: src/renderables/error.rs

```rust
//! Error panel renderable for beautiful error display.

use crate::theme::Theme;

#[cfg(feature = "rich")]
use rich_rust::prelude::*;

/// Error severity level for styling.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum ErrorSeverity {
    /// Critical error - red, urgent
    Critical,
    /// Standard error - red
    Error,
    /// Warning - yellow
    Warning,
    /// Notice - cyan (informational)
    Notice,
}

/// A panel specifically designed for error display.
pub struct ErrorPanel {
    /// Error severity for styling
    severity: ErrorSeverity,
    /// Panel title (e.g., "SQL Syntax Error")
    title: String,
    /// Main error message
    message: String,
    /// Optional SQL query that caused the error
    sql: Option<String>,
    /// Position in SQL where error occurred (1-indexed)
    sql_position: Option<usize>,
    /// SQLSTATE code (PostgreSQL error code)
    sqlstate: Option<String>,
    /// Additional detail from database
    detail: Option<String>,
    /// Hint for fixing the error
    hint: Option<String>,
    /// Additional context lines
    context: Vec<String>,
}

impl ErrorPanel {
    /// Create a new error panel with title and message.
    pub fn new(title: impl Into<String>, message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        Self {
            severity: ErrorSeverity::Error,
            title: title.into(),
            message: message.into(),
            sql: None,
            sql_position: None,
            sqlstate: None,
            detail: None,
            hint: None,
            context: Vec::new(),
        }
    }
    
    /// Set error severity.
    pub fn severity(mut self, severity: ErrorSeverity) -> Self {
        self.severity = severity;
        self
    }
    
    /// Add SQL query context.
    pub fn with_sql(mut self, sql: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        self.sql = Some(sql.into());
        self
    }
    
    /// Add error position in SQL (1-indexed character position).
    pub fn with_position(mut self, position: usize) -> Self {
        self.sql_position = Some(position);
        self
    }
    
    /// Add SQLSTATE code.
    pub fn with_sqlstate(mut self, code: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        self.sqlstate = Some(code.into());
        self
    }
    
    /// Add detail message.
    pub fn with_detail(mut self, detail: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        self.detail = Some(detail.into());
        self
    }
    
    /// Add hint for fixing.
    pub fn with_hint(mut self, hint: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        self.hint = Some(hint.into());
        self
    }
    
    /// Add context line.
    pub fn add_context(mut self, line: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        self.context.push(line.into());
        self
    }
    
    /// Render as rich Panel (feature = "rich").
    #[cfg(feature = "rich")]
    pub fn to_panel(&self, theme: &Theme) -> Panel {
        let border_color = match self.severity {
            ErrorSeverity::Critical => theme.error.to_color(),
            ErrorSeverity::Error => theme.error.to_color(),
            ErrorSeverity::Warning => theme.warning.to_color(),
            ErrorSeverity::Notice => theme.info.to_color(),
        };
        
        let mut content = Text::new(&self.message);
        content.append_line("");
        
        // SQL context with position marker
        if let Some(ref sql) = self.sql {
            content.append_line("┌─ Query ─────────────────────────────────────────┐");
            content.append_line(format!("│ {}", sql));
            
            if let Some(pos) = self.sql_position {
                let marker = format!("│ {}^", " ".repeat(pos.saturating_sub(1)));
                content.append_line(marker);
            }
            content.append_line("└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘");
            content.append_line("");
        }
        
        // Detail
        if let Some(ref detail) = self.detail {
            content.append_line(format!("Detail: {}", detail));
        }
        
        // Hint
        if let Some(ref hint) = self.hint {
            content.append_line("");
            content.append(format!("💡 Hint: {}", hint), theme.info.to_style());
        }
        
        // SQLSTATE
        if let Some(ref code) = self.sqlstate {
            content.append_line("");
            content.append(format!("SQLSTATE: {}", code), theme.dim.to_style());
        }
        
        // Context
        for line in &self.context {
            content.append_line(line);
        }
        
        Panel::from_rich_text(content)
            .title(&self.title)
            .title_style(Style::new().bold().color(border_color.clone()))
            .border_style(Style::new().color(border_color))
    }
    
    /// Render as plain text.
    pub fn to_plain(&self) -> String {
        let mut lines = Vec::new();
        
        lines.push(format!("=== {} ===", self.title));
        lines.push(String::new());
        lines.push(self.message.clone());
        
        if let Some(ref sql) = self.sql {
            lines.push(String::new());
            lines.push("Query:".to_string());
            lines.push(format!("  {}", sql));
            
            if let Some(pos) = self.sql_position {
                lines.push(format!("  {}^", " ".repeat(pos.saturating_sub(1))));
            }
        }
        
        if let Some(ref detail) = self.detail {
            lines.push(String::new());
            lines.push(format!("Detail: {}", detail));
        }
        
        if let Some(ref hint) = self.hint {
            lines.push(String::new());
            lines.push(format!("Hint: {}", hint));
        }
        
        if let Some(ref code) = self.sqlstate {
            lines.push(String::new());
            lines.push(format!("SQLSTATE: {}", code));
        }
        
        for line in &self.context {
            lines.push(line.clone());
        }
        
        lines.join("\n")
    }
    
    /// Render as JSON-serializable structure.
    pub fn to_json(&self) -> serde_json::Value {
        serde_json::json!({
            "severity": format!("{:?}", self.severity),
            "title": self.title,
            "message": self.message,
            "sql": self.sql,
            "position": self.sql_position,
            "sqlstate": self.sqlstate,
            "detail": self.detail,
            "hint": self.hint,
            "context": self.context,
        })
    }
}
```

## Usage Example

```rust
let panel = ErrorPanel::new("SQL Syntax Error", "Unexpected token 'SELCT'")
    .with_sql("SELCT * FROM users WHERE id = $1")
    .with_position(1)
    .with_sqlstate("42601")
    .with_hint("Did you mean 'SELECT'?");

// Rich mode
console.print_renderable(&panel.to_panel(&theme));

// Plain mode
println!("{}", panel.to_plain());
```

## Verification

```bash
cargo check -p sqlmodel-console --features rich
cargo test -p sqlmodel-console renderables::error::tests
cargo run --example error_demo --features rich
```","acceptance_criteria":"ErrorPanel renders errors with context and suggestions
Panel includes file path and line number when available
Panel includes relevant SQL snippet when available

 {"id":"bd-emz","title":"Session API: Complete All Methods","description":"## Overview

Implement ALL Session methods from Python SQLModel/SQLAlchemy.

## Required Session Methods

### Core CRUD
- add(instance) - Add object to session ✅
- add_all(instances) - Add multiple objects
- delete(instance) - Mark for deletion ✅
- get(entity, ident, ...) - Get by primary key
- merge(instance, load=True) - Merge detached instance

### Transaction Control
- commit() - Commit transaction ✅
- rollback() - Rollback transaction ✅
- flush(objects=None) - Flush pending changes ✅
- begin() - Begin transaction ✅
- begin_nested() - Savepoint ✅
- close() - Close session

### State Management
- refresh(instance, attribute_names=None) - Refresh from DB
- expire(instance, attribute_names=None) - Expire instance cache
- expire_all() - Expire all instances
- expunge(instance) - Remove from session ✅
- expunge_all() - Remove all from session
- is_modified(instance, ...) - Check if modified

### Query Execution
- exec(statement, ...) - Execute and return models
- execute(statement, ...) - Low-level execute (deprecated in Python)
- scalars(statement, ...) - Return scalar values

## Missing/Incomplete Methods

1. **add_all** - Bulk add
2. **get** - Primary key lookup (different from query)
3. **merge** - Merge detached objects back
4. **expire/expire_all** - Cache invalidation
5. **is_modified** - Dirty checking
6. **refresh** - Reload from database

## Unit of Work Pattern

Python SQLAlchemy tracks:
- New objects (pending insert)
- Dirty objects (pending update)
- Deleted objects (pending delete)
- Identity map (object cache by PK)

We need to implement this fully for proper ORM behavior.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:59:40.729795660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T16:58:20.518988753Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-emz","depends_on_id":"bd-162","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:58:20.518961162Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
